isolation.scope: Single isolated agent-browser session against the shared localhost:3001 app; only in-browser game state was mutated.

assertions.title: Promotion Dialog
assertions.reason: Blocked by the lack of a user-surface position editor/FEN loader; repeated real-game setup attempts never reached a promotable white pawn position in bounded validation time.

steps.action: Inspect the available controls on a fresh page load
steps.expected: A user path exists to set up or reach promotion through the public UI
steps.observed: The public UI exposed theme selection, difficulty, Undo, and New Game only; no position editor or import control existed.

steps.action: Try real-game setup lines on Easy difficulty while staying within the public UI
steps.expected: A deterministic path reaches a promotion-ready pawn
steps.observed: Repeated engine-driven lines diverged before any white pawn reached the 7th rank, so the promotion prerequisite could not be established reliably.

assertions.issues: Prerequisite not reachable through the available user surface during isolated validation: no UI to seed a near-promotion position, and engine-driven live play did not produce one.

assertions.title: Castling Animates Both Pieces
assertions.reason: Pass — after clearing f1/g1, clicking the white king from e1 to g1 placed the king on g1 and rook on f1, and play continued normally.

steps.action: Start an Easy-difficulty game and play 1.e4 e6 2.Nf3 c5 3.Bc4 Nc6
steps.expected: White kingside castling becomes legal with f1 and g1 cleared
steps.observed: The pre-castle board showed the bishop on c4 and knight on f3 with the king still on e1 and rook on h1.

steps.action: Switch to Dark Marble and castle by selecting the king on e1 then square g1
steps.expected: The UI moves both the king and rook to their castled squares
steps.observed: The resulting board showed the white king on g1 and white rook on f1; the game then continued with Black replying ...d5.

assertions.title: En Passant Removes Correct Pawn
assertions.reason: Blocked by the prerequisite board state: repeated real-user opening lines never produced an adjacent black double-pawn push next to an advanced white pawn with an immediate en-passant capture available.

steps.expected: Black eventually offers ...d5 or ...f5 so White can capture en passant
steps.observed: The engine chose ...Nc6 and ...Bb4 instead; no en-passant target square appeared for the white e5-pawn.

steps.expected: Black offers ...d5 or ...b5 so White can capture en passant
steps.observed: The engine replied ...Bxc5, removing the advanced pawn before any en-passant opportunity existed.

assertions.issues: Prerequisite not reproducible through the current public UI in bounded time because the app offers no board editor/FEN loader and the engine's replies did not expose a live en-passant capture.

assertions.title: Three Board Themes Available
assertions.reason: Pass — Classic Wood, Dark Marble, and Light Minimalist were all present and produced visibly distinct light/dark square colors.

steps.action: Load the app and inspect the Board theme controls
steps.observed: The UI exposed Classic Wood, Dark Marble, and Light Minimalist theme buttons.

steps.action: Capture each theme and compare board square colors
steps.expected: Each theme uses clearly different square colors
steps.observed: Classic Wood used warm tan/brown squares, Dark Marble used cream/charcoal squares, and Light Minimalist used near-white/slate squares.

assertions.title: Theme Switching
assertions.reason: Pass — switching themes updated the board immediately without a reload, while board placement, move count, evaluation, and last move remained intact.

steps.action: Reach an active game state after 1.e4 e6 2.Nf3 c5 3.Bc4 Nc6
steps.expected: A non-starting position is visible before switching themes
steps.observed: The board placement was r1bqkbnr/pp1p1ppp/2n1p3/2p5/2B1P3/5N2/PPPP1PPP/RNBQK2R with 6 half-moves played.

steps.action: Click Dark Marble, then later click Light Minimalist
steps.expected: Theme colors change instantly and the live game state is preserved
steps.observed: The URL stayed at http://localhost:3001/, the pre-switch placement remained unchanged after the Dark Marble switch, and the later Light Minimalist view still preserved move history, captures, and the active position.

assertions.title: Theme Switch During Active Game
assertions.reason: Pass — after 6 half-moves, the theme switched during live play with the same placement preserved, and the game continued normally into 4.O-O d5.

steps.expected: An active game with 5+ moves is in progress
steps.observed: The board had 6 half-moves, no captures, and last move Nc6.

steps.action: Switch from Classic Wood to Dark Marble during the active game, then continue with O-O
steps.expected: Theme changes immediately while position, history, captures, and evaluation are preserved, and play can continue
steps.observed: The placement before and after the switch stayed identical, captures remained empty, move history was preserved, and after castling the game continued with Black's ...d5 reply.

frictions.description: Board square buttons were covered by piece buttons, so moving pieces reliably required clicking the piece button first and then the destination square.
frictions.resolution: Used piece-button clicks followed by destination-square clicks for all move setup flows.

frictions.description: The public UI has no board editor or FEN loader, so promotion and en-passant prerequisite positions depend entirely on live engine cooperation.
frictions.resolution: Worked around it by trying multiple Easy-difficulty real-game setup lines, but the required board states were still not reached deterministically.

blockers.description: Special-move prerequisite positions for promotion and en passant were not reproducible through the available user surface in bounded validation time.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Ran multiple Easy-difficulty live setup lines (including 1.e4 e6 2.e5 and 1.c4 e5 2.c5) but the engine never exposed a promotion-ready pawn or a live en-passant capture.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 4 passed and 2 were blocked. Theme availability/switching, mid-game theme preservation, and white kingside castling all worked with no console/runtime errors; promotion and en-passant remained blocked because the current UI exposes no deterministic position setup path and live engine play did not yield those prerequisites.
